content.thinking: Let me look at the helpers/compile/build.ts to understand the build system and how `banner` is used:

content.content:
1	import { ChokidarOptions, watch as createWatcher } from 'chokidar'
2	import * as esbuild from 'esbuild'
3	import { BuildContext } from 'esbuild'
4	import { writeFileSync } from 'fs'
5	import glob from 'globby'
6	import path from 'path'
7	
8	import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
9	import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
10	import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
11	import { map } from '../blaze/map'
12	import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'
13	import { pipe } from '../blaze/pipe'
14	import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
15	import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
16	import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
17	import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
18	import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'
19	
20	export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
21	export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
22	  name?: string
23	  emitTypes?: boolean
24	  emitMetafile?: boolean
25	  outbase?: never // we don't support this
26	}
27	
28	const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
29	  platform: 'node',
30	  target: 'ES2022',
31	  logLevel: 'error',
32	  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
33	  metafile: true,
34	} as const
35	
36	/**
37	 * Apply defaults to the original build options
38	 * @param options the original build options
39	 */
40	const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
41	  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
42	  format: 'cjs',
43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
44	  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
45	  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
46	    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
47	  }),
48	  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
49	  ...options,
50	  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
51	  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
58	    onErrorPlugin,
59	  ],
60	  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
61	})
62	
63	/**
64	 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * We only want to trigger the glob search once we are ready, and that is when
82	 * the previous build has finished. We get the build options from the deferred.
83	 */
84	function computeOptions(options: () => BuildOptions) {
85	  return options()
86	}
87	
88	/**
89	 * Extensions are not automatically by esbuild set for `options.outfile`. We
90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */
92	function addExtensionFormat(options: BuildOptions) {
93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`
97	  }
98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`
104	 */
105	function addDefaultOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path
207	function getOutFile(options: BuildOptions) {
208	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)
210	    const filename = path.basename(options.outfile)
211	
212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []
232	}
233

3	import { BuildOptions } from './build'
4	
5	type AdapterEntry = {
6	  entry: string
7	  outfile: string
8	}
9	
10	export function createAdapterConfig(entries: AdapterEntry[]): BuildOptions[] {
11	  return entries.flatMap(({ entry, outfile }) => {
12	    const baseName = path.basename(outfile)
13	
14	    return [
15	      {
16	        name: `${baseName}-cjs`,
17	        format: 'cjs' as const,
18	        bundle: true,
19	        entryPoints: [entry],
20	        outfile,
21	        outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
22	        emitTypes: true,
23	      },
24	      {
25	        name: `${baseName}-esm`,
26	        format: 'esm' as const,
27	        bundle: true,
28	        entryPoints: [entry],
29	        outfile,
30	        outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
31	        emitTypes: true,
32	      },
33	    ]
34	  })
35	}
36	
37	export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }])
38	
39	export const unbundledConfig: BuildOptions[] = [
40	  {
41	    name: 'cjs',
42	    format: 'cjs',
43	    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
44	    outdir: 'dist',
45	    outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
46	    emitTypes: true,
47	  },
48	  {
49	    name: 'esm',
50	    format: 'esm',
51	    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
52	    outdir: 'dist',
53	    outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
54	    emitTypes: true,
55	  },
56	]

file.content:
import { ReadonlyDeep } from './util'

export type Document = ReadonlyDeep<{
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}>

export type Mappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
  otherOperations: {
    read: string[]
    write: string[]
  }
}>

export type OtherOperationMappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  read: string[]
  write: string[]
}>

export type DatamodelEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: EnumValue[]
  dbName?: string | null
  documentation?: string
}>

export type SchemaEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: string[]
}>

export type EnumValue = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
}>

export type Datamodel = ReadonlyDeep<{
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}>

export type uniqueIndex = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: string[]
}>
export type PrimaryKey = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string | null
  fields: string[]
}>
export type Model = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}>

export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'

export type FieldNamespace = 'model' | 'prisma'
export type FieldLocation = 'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'

export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
  kind: FieldKind
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  /**
   * Describes the data type in the same the way it is defined in the Prisma schema:
   * BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, Decimal, Float, Int, JSON, String, $ModelName
   */
  type: string
  /**
   * Native database type, if specified.
   * For example, `@db.VarChar(191)` is encoded as `['VarChar', ['191']]`,
   * `@db.Text` is encoded as `['Text', []]`.
   */
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
}>

export type FieldDefault = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  args: Array<string | number>
}>

export type FieldDefaultScalar = string | boolean | number

export type Index = ReadonlyDeep<{
  model: string
  type: IndexType
  isDefinedOnField: boolean
  name?: string
  dbName?: string
  algorithm?: string
  clustered?: boolean
  fields: IndexField[]
}>

content.thinking:
Now I have enough information to start implementing the package. Let me create all the necessary files.

Let me plan the files I need to create:

1. `packages/generator-rest-client/package.json` - Following the `@prisma/debug` pattern
2. `packages/generator-rest-client/tsconfig.json` - Identical to `packages/debug/tsconfig.json`
3. `packages/generator-rest-client/tsconfig.build.json` - Identical to `packages/debug/tsconfig.build.json`


4. `packages/generator-rest-client/helpers/build.ts` - Custom CJS-only build with shebang
5. `packages/generator-rest-client/src/index.ts` - Entry point with generatorHandler
6. `packages/generator-rest-client/src/generator.ts` - Main generation logic
7. `packages/generator-rest-client/src/template.ts` - Template helpers

I also need to modify `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` to add the path mapping. For the build.ts file, since this is a binary generator, I want a single CommonJS entry point with a shebang at the top to make it executable.

Setting up package.json... Setting up the generator handler that registers the manifest and processes generation options, then implementing the core generate function that reads the output directory from the options and prepares to render the REST client template.

Now I need to build out the template module that will handle the actual code generation—mapping Prisma scalar types to TypeScript, rendering enums and model interfaces while filtering out object-kind fields, and creating CRUD methods for the client. Writing the type conversion logic... Building the CRUD client methods... Finishing the delete method error handling, then wrapping up the client generation function that iterates through all models to create their respective CRUD clients.

Now I'm thinking through the build configuration more carefully—since `BuildOptions` extends esbuild's options, the banner property with a shebang is valid, and for the generator binary I need a single CommonJS output bundled together with an outfile path. I'm realizing that when `bundle: true` is set, the build system automatically marks regular dependencies as external, which would prevent `@prisma/generator-helper` from being bundled into our generator binary. Since we need everything bundled together, I need to figure out how to either bundle the helper or configure it differently than a standard dependency. it, we could use `IGNORE_EXTERNALS` env var or put it in `devDependencies`.

Looking at the `getProjectExternals` function, when `bundle === true`, dependencies get marked as external. To bundle `@prisma/generator-helper`, I'd need to either put it in `devDependencies`, use an `external` override, or set `IGNORE_EXTERNALS`. Since this is a generator binary that should be self-contained, putting `@prisma/generator-helper` in `devDependencies` makes more sense.

Now I'm checking how other generator packages in the monorepo handle this pattern, since the plan mentions following the `@prisma/debug` approach with a bin entry.

3	/** Maps Prisma scalar type names to their TypeScript equivalents. */
4	const PRISMA_SCALAR_TO_TS: Record<string, string> = {
5	  String: 'string',
6	  Boolean: 'boolean',
7	  Int: 'number',
8	  Float: 'number',
9	  BigInt: 'bigint',
10	  Decimal: 'string',
11	  DateTime: 'string',
12	  Json: 'unknown',
13	  Bytes: 'string',
14	}
15	
16	/**
17	 * Converts a DMMF scalar or enum field type to its TypeScript representation,
18	 * accounting for list and optional modifiers.
19	 */
20	export function prismaFieldToTs(field: DMMF.Field): string {
21	  let baseType: string
22	
23	  if (field.kind === 'enum') {
24	    baseType = field.type
25	  } else if (field.kind === 'scalar') {
26	    baseType = PRISMA_SCALAR_TO_TS[field.type] ?? 'unknown'
27	  } else {
28	    baseType = 'unknown'
29	  }
30	
31	  const withList = field.isList ? baseType + '[]' : baseType
32	  return field.isRequired ? withList : withList + ' | null'
33	}
34	
35	/** Renders a TypeScript `enum` declaration for a Prisma datamodel enum. */
36	export function renderEnum(e: DMMF.DatamodelEnum): string {
37	  const values = e.values.map((v) => '  ' + v.name + " = '" + v.name + "',").join('\n')
38	  return 'export enum ' + e.name + ' {\n' + values + '\n}'
39	}
40	
41	/**
42	 * Renders a TypeScript `interface` for a Prisma model, including only scalar
43	 * and enum fields (relation fields are skipped to avoid circular references).
44	 */
45	export function renderModelInterface(model: DMMF.Model): string {
46	  const fields = model.fields.filter((f) => f.kind !== 'object')
47	  const lines = fields.map((f) => {
48	    const optionalMark = f.isRequired ? '' : '?'
49	    const tsType = prismaFieldToTs(f)
50	    return '  ' + f.name + optionalMark + ': ' + tsType
51	  })
52	  return 'export interface ' + model.name + ' {\n' + lines.join('\n') + '\n}'
53	}
54	
55	/**
56	 * Renders a `const` object with typed CRUD methods (`findMany`, `findUnique`,
57	 * `create`, `update`, `delete`) for a given Prisma model. Each method accepts
58	 * a `baseUrl` and uses the global `fetch` API.
59	 */
60	export function renderModelClient(model: DMMF.Model): string {
61	  const name = model.name
62	  const resourcePath = lcFirst(name)
63	
64	  const idField = model.fields.find((f) => f.isId)
65	  const idName = idField?.name ?? 'id'
66	  const idType = idField ? prismaFieldToTs(idField) : 'string'
67	
68	  const lines: string[] = [
69	    'export const ' + resourcePath + 'Client = {',
70	
71	    '  async findMany(baseUrl: string): Promise<' + name + '[]> {',
72	    "    const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/" + resourcePath + '`)',
73	    "    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`GET /" + resourcePath + ' failed: ${res.statusText}`)',
74	    '    return res.json() as Promise<' + name + '[]>',
75	    '  },',
76	    '',
77	
78	    '  async findUnique(baseUrl: string, ' + idName + ': ' + idType + '): Promise<' + name + ' | null> {',
79	    '    const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/' + resourcePath + '/${' + idName + '}`,)',
80	    '    if (res.status === 404) return null',
81	    "    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`GET /" + resourcePath + '/${' + idName + '} failed: ${res.statusText}`)',
82	    '    return res.json() as Promise<' + name + '>',
83	    '  },',
84	    '',
85	
86	    '  async create(baseUrl: string, data: Omit<' + name + ", '" + idName + "'>): Promise<" + name + '> {',
87	    '    const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/' + resourcePath + '`, {',
88	    "      method: 'POST',",
89	    "      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },",
90	    '      body: JSON.stringify(data),',
91	    '    })',
92	    "    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`POST /" + resourcePath + ' failed: ${res.statusText}`)',
93	    '    return res.json() as Promise<' + name + '>',
94	    '  },',
95	    '',
96	
97	    '  async update(baseUrl: string, ' + idName + ': ' + idType + ', data: Partial<' + name + '>): Promise<' + name + '> {',
98	    '    const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/' + resourcePath + '/${' + idName + '}`, {',
99	    "      method: 'PATCH',",
100	    "      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },",
101	    '      body: JSON.stringify(data),',
102	    '    })',
103	    "    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`PATCH /" + resourcePath + '/${' + idName + '} failed: ${res.statusText}`)',
104	    '    return res.json() as Promise<' + name + '>',
105	    '  },',
106	    '',
107	
108	    '  async delete(baseUrl: string, ' + idName + ': ' + idType + '): Promise<void> {',
109	    "    const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/" + resourcePath + '/${' + idName + '}`, { method: \'DELETE\' })',
110	    "    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`DELETE /" + resourcePath + '/${' + idName + '} failed: ${res.statusText}`)',
111	    '  },',
112	
113	    '}',
114	  ]
115	
116	  return lines.join('\n')
117	}
118	
119	/** Renders the complete `rest-client.ts` output from a DMMF document. */
120	export function renderRestClient(dmmf: DMMF.Document): string {
121	  const sections: string[] = ['// Auto-generated by @prisma/generator-rest-client. Do not edit.', '']
122	
123	  for (const e of dmmf.datamodel.enums) {
124	    sections.push(renderEnum(e))
125	    sections.push('')
126	  }
127	
128	  for (const model of dmmf.datamodel.models) {
129	    sections.push(renderModelInterface(model))
130	    sections.push('')
131	  }
132	
133	  for (const model of dmmf.datamodel.models) {
134	    sections.push(renderModelClient(model))
135	    sections.push('')
136	  }
137	
138	  return sections.join('\n')
139	}
140	
141	function lcFirst(str: string): string {
142	  return str.charAt(0).toLowerCase() + str.slice(1)
143	}
